stemHtml: While flying in the NAT HLA, a flight crew receives a clearance which differs from their logged flight plan. What is the risk associated with this?

options.a: The only way to change the flight plan is manually, which can lead to errors in inputting the data.
options.b: A significant proportion of navigation errors investigated in the NAT involve an aircraft which has followed its flight plan rather than its differing clearance.
options.c: The crew should report that they are unable to follow the clearance, and can only operate along the original flight plan to prevent navigation errors.
options.d: The crew can only accept the clearance if the new routing can be flown using ground navigation aids to verify position errors.
